title: ophrys × apicula nothosubsp. fabrei
intro: Ophrys × apicula nothosubsp. fabrei, also known as the hybrid bee orchid, is a man-made, sterile, and rare orchid species resulting from a cross between Ophrys apifera and Ophrys insectifera. The plant is believed to possess medicinal properties due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been studied for their potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ophrys × apicula nothosubsp. fabrei is used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from its dried roots and leaves, and are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures.
